title: 8 Small Games in 1 Small Box - Travel Gaming Update
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1k6Bzkiw48o

Daniel (@dungeondive) revisits his ultra-compact travel gaming box—the One Card Dungeon case now stuffed with eight different games by swapping the large dice for smaller ones. The collection includes Corg Classic Volume 1 (a business-card-sized adventure game where you defeat monsters and shop for gear), two copies of Squire for Hire (spatial backpack management that plays two players), the original nine-card Mini Rogue, Advanced Quest variants, Business Card Dungeon Dive, and the base One Card Dungeon.

For those wanting something meatier, Daniel recommends The Crows of Copper Shell Bay (a Tiny Tussle in the Poppers Ladder universe), Pocket Hex (a hex-crawl adventure in a box with pencil included), and Kingdom Legacy (a legacy campaign that fits in your pocket—though he notes it uses AI art, disclosed on the BGG page). The goal: games you can decompress with after a brutal conference day, not deep strategic experiences. Sometimes you just need to roll a d6 and fight a skeleton in a hotel room.

----

What's the smallest complete gaming experience you've managed to pack for travel?
